title: Diatribe Of A Mad Housewife

jokes.text: d-ohh! (Screams)
jokes.setup: Opening moments of any Simpsons episode
jokes.punchline: Homer's signature catchphrase followed by screaming
jokes.explanation: Iconic Homer catchphrase, perfect character integration but no originality since it's used every episode

jokes.text: Let's see. I'll have 16 gravy scrape 'ems, a bucket of twisty lard, and two super-choker breakfast burritos with macho sauce.
jokes.punchline: The ridiculously unhealthy and creatively named menu items
jokes.explanation: Classic Simpsons satire of fast food with inventive fake product names that feel both absurd and believable

jokes.text: I could also deep-fry the bag.
jokes.punchline: The teen worker suggesting an even more ridiculous addition
jokes.explanation: Good escalation joke that pushes the fast food absurdity further with perfect economy

jokes.text: Great. And I'll have a diet coke-- deep-fried.
jokes.setup: Ordering a diet drink after all that unhealthy food
jokes.punchline: Requesting it deep-fried, negating any health benefit
jokes.explanation: Perfect character comedy - Homer's illogical approach to 'health' food with brilliant economy

jokes.text: need more lap.
jokes.setup: Homer eating massive amounts of food in his car
jokes.punchline: His stomach has grown so large he needs more lap space
jokes.explanation: Excellent economy - two words perfectly capture Homer's predicament and gluttony

jokes.text: I'll have to steer by the reflection in my watch!
jokes.punchline: His bizarre solution involving his watch reflection
jokes.explanation: Creative absurdist logic that feels uniquely Homer-esque

jokes.text: Why don't I just pull over?
jokes.punchline: His sudden realization of the obvious safe choice
jokes.explanation: Perfect Homer moment - overthinking then finding the obvious solution

jokes.text: can't stop! Krusty collectible toy jammed under brake pedal!
jokes.punchline: The ridiculously specific and brand-appropriate reason
jokes.explanation: Good world-building humor that connects to the show's universe while being absurd

jokes.text: intruder detected. Calling local police. (busy signal) line busy. Shutting down.
jokes.punchline: System gives up when police line is busy
jokes.explanation: Good tech satire with logical absurdity - what happens when systems fail

jokes.text: We're well-protected against every threat, from bomb-toting bolsheviks to golden-armed jazzbos.
jokes.punchline: His antiquated and absurd threat categories
jokes.explanation: Perfect Burns character writing - his outdated worldview creates perfect absurdist threats

jokes.text: Oh, no! I hit the grief counselor!
jokes.punchline: The victim is specifically someone who helps with trauma
jokes.explanation: Dark irony that works because of the specific profession of the victim

jokes.text: Promise... me... you'll report this.
jokes.punchline: He's still being responsible and helpful even while dying
jokes.explanation: Excellent dark character comedy - even dying, he maintains professional ethics

jokes.text: Consider yourself fired!
jokes.punchline: His inappropriate response to Homer killing someone
jokes.explanation: Perfect Burns character moment - his priorities are completely wrong

jokes.text: I had a car!
jokes.setup: Homer just killed someone and got fired
jokes.punchline: His concern about losing his car
jokes.explanation: Good Homer priorities joke - missing the big picture for minor concerns

jokes.text: I'm gonna taunt the phd'S.
jokes.punchline: His specific plan to harass academics
jokes.explanation: Good Bart characterization - his mischievous nature applied to academic settings

jokes.text: Hey, guys, I heard an assistant professorship just opened up. Oh! Where? Ooh! At the university of... psyche!
jokes.setup: Bart offering hope about job prospects
jokes.punchline: The cruel 'psyche' reversal crushing their hopes
jokes.explanation: Classic setup/punchline structure with cruel but character-appropriate execution

jokes.text: When it happens in a book, it's romantic. But, when willie tries to kiss ya, you're all pepper spray and fingernails.
jokes.punchline: His complaint about double standards in his own romantic attempts
jokes.explanation: Excellent Willie character moment - his crude romantic observations with perfect voice

jokes.text: Marge simpson, longtime reader, first-time stander-upper.
jokes.setup: Marge introducing herself at book reading
jokes.punchline: Adapting radio show format to bookstore Q&A
jokes.explanation: Nice wordplay that shows Marge's nervousness and politeness

jokes.text: Just a class at the 'y'-- yale university.
jokes.setup: Author mentions taking a class at 'the Y'
jokes.punchline: Reveals it's Yale, not the YMCA
jokes.explanation: Good misdirection with efficient setup and payoff

jokes.text: If I write a book, will they tell me when it comes out?
jokes.setup: Discussion about anyone being able to write
jokes.punchline: Marge's hilariously basic concern about the publishing process
jokes.explanation: Perfect Marge naivety - focusing on logistics rather than creative challenges

jokes.text: I'll steal this sign, and they'll pay me to make them a new one!
jokes.setup: Homer needing a new job quickly
jokes.punchline: His 'create your own demand' business plan
jokes.explanation: Good Homer logic - criminal but with entrepreneurial thinking

jokes.text: We'll clean out those stupid americans.
jokes.punchline: Their open contempt for American customers
jokes.explanation: Standard stereotype humor that feels dated and not particularly clever

jokes.text: Did that salesman cut one during the test drive? Yeah. And, for some reason, he turned on the radio to cover up the smell.
jokes.setup: Couple discussing their car buying experience
jokes.punchline: The illogical attempt to mask a smell with sound
jokes.explanation: Decent absurdist humor about confused senses

jokes.text: I bet a lot of hippies were denied care in this thing. Injuhippies.
jokes.punchline: His portmanteau word about hippie medical discrimination
jokes.explanation: Creative wordplay that combines social commentary with Homer's casual callousness

jokes.text: Buy me! Buy me! Buy me!
jokes.setup: Ambulance siren is the only thing that works
jokes.punchline: The siren seeming to beg Homer to purchase
jokes.explanation: Simple anthropomorphization gag that works for the moment

jokes.text: You work at the nuclear plant. Get with the program, marge. Your husband is now an ambulance driver.
jokes.punchline: Homer's confident dismissal of his previous career
jokes.explanation: Good Homer character moment showing his casual approach to major life changes

jokes.text: Maybe on planet zoozoo.
jokes.setup: Marge asking if ambulance driving needs training
jokes.punchline: Homer's dismissive response with made-up planet
jokes.explanation: Classic Homer dismissiveness with silly made-up reference

jokes.text: Hey, I think it's cool. I can defibrillate lisa! Not if I pump your heart full of morphine first!
jokes.punchline: Lisa's darker escalation of the medical threat
jokes.explanation: Good sibling rivalry escalation with dark medical humor

jokes.text: Those aren't your toys. They're to keep daddy asleep and awake.
jokes.punchline: Marge's matter-of-fact description of stimulants and depressants
jokes.explanation: Excellent Marge line - casually explaining serious drugs in childlike terms

jokes.text: Homer, you left two jobs and bought an ambulance without even a phone call. I also fed some ducklings. I know. I got your message.
jokes.punchline: Homer adding duckling feeding as if it's equivalent, and Marge's casual acknowledgment
jokes.explanation: Excellent absurdist humor showing Homer's skewed priorities and Marge's resignation

jokes.text: I'm writing a novel! Whatever. But I think you look great already.
jokes.setup: Marge correcting Homer about hair appointment
jokes.punchline: Homer still thinking it's about her appearance
jokes.explanation: Good character comedy showing Homer's inattentiveness and reflexive compliments

jokes.text: A novel about whaling. That's something you haven't seen before.
jokes.setup: Marge deciding on her novel topic
jokes.punchline: Her obliviousness to Moby-Dick's existence
jokes.explanation: Perfect irony - Marge unknowingly reinventing one of literature's most famous works

jokes.text: 'Swim, swim, swim,' thought the whale, flapping his... floppers.
jokes.punchline: Her amateur writing style and made-up whale anatomy
jokes.explanation: Good amateur writer parody with Marge's earnest but clumsy prose

jokes.text: Mayor quimby, disco stu, and our fighting men and women overseas. Well, I finished the 'thank yous.'
jokes.punchline: Her extensive acknowledgments before writing the actual book
jokes.explanation: Good procrastination humor with random thank-you list including Disco Stu

jokes.text: Temperance barrows stared at the sea like a dog stares at a ham.
jokes.setup: Marge writing her first real paragraph
jokes.punchline: Her amateurish but endearing simile
jokes.explanation: Charming amateur writing parody - bad but earnest literary attempt

jokes.text: For the third time, the hospital. You're an ambulance, not a taxi!
jokes.setup: Patient trying to get to hospital
jokes.punchline: Homer treating ambulance like taxi service
jokes.explanation: Good incompetence humor showing Homer's confusion about his job

jokes.text: Why don't you just admit you don't know where the hospital is. Why don't you admit I know it's around here somewhere!
jokes.punchline: Homer's illogical defensive response
jokes.explanation: Classic Homer defensive stubbornness with nonsensical logic

jokes.text: For temperance, the days passed on... as did seven of her ten children.
jokes.setup: Marge writing about passage of time
jokes.punchline: Casual mention of massive child mortality
jokes.explanation: Brilliant dark humor with perfect structure - casual mention of tragedy as a throwaway detail

jokes.text: This family has not seen whale meat for a onemonth.
jokes.punchline: Marge's made-up time period 'onemonth'
jokes.explanation: Minor wordplay humor showing Marge's amateur writing style

jokes.text: tobacco.
jokes.punchline: The miracle crop is tobacco - now known to be deadly
jokes.explanation: Dark historical irony with perfect economy - one word reveals the tragedy

jokes.text: Ooh, it has colored my teeth a healthy brown.
jokes.setup: Character receiving tobacco as a gift
jokes.punchline: Describing tooth staining as 'healthy'
jokes.explanation: Good period-appropriate irony about tobacco being considered healthy

jokes.text: We're not paramedics! I'll say.
jokes.setup: Patient pointing out they're not paramedics
jokes.punchline: Homer's enthusiastic agreement with the complaint
jokes.explanation: Good Homer incompetence moment - agreeing with criticism of himself

jokes.text: Temperance had to face the unhappy truth she had married a brute. Hey, baby. I've returned from portsmouth. Now let me put my tongue down yourmouth.
jokes.setup: Marge writing about her character's realization
jokes.punchline: The crude husband character that clearly represents Homer
jokes.explanation: Excellent meta-humor - Marge unconsciously writing about her own marriage problems

jokes.text: a seagull? The whales weren't biting, okay?
jokes.punchline: It's just a seagull instead of whale
jokes.explanation: Good incompetence humor paralleling Homer's work failures

jokes.text: This story is as dark as those new milky way bars. Ooh, that's a good analogy. I'll work it in somewhere.
jokes.punchline: Her pride in the candy bar comparison and plan to include it
jokes.explanation: Clever meta-humor about amateur writing and product placement in literature

jokes.text: If so, then I guess I'm just a cave man. If they existed, which they didn'T.
jokes.punchline: His religious disclaimer about evolution
jokes.explanation: Excellent Flanders character moment - perfectly capturing his fundamentalist worldview in casual conversation

jokes.text: Nantucket is an island? What do you know? It is!
jokes.punchline: Marge's surprise at discovering basic geography while writing
jokes.explanation: Good amateur writer humor - learning basic facts while creating her story

jokes.text: Hey, baby, I know we work together, but I think you're hot.
jokes.punchline: His inappropriate workplace behavior
jokes.explanation: Standard cringe humor showing the husband's boorishness

jokes.text: prithee, tell me thou art not a sodomite. Nope, not even a gomorrahian.
jokes.punchline: The made-up term 'Gomorrahian' as a parallel to Sodomite
jokes.explanation: Clever biblical wordplay that works both as period dialogue and modern humor

jokes.text: I've got an overpowering urge to see you... with your hat off.
jokes.punchline: The tame reveal of wanting to see her hatless
jokes.explanation: Excellent misdirection playing with romance novel expectations vs. period modesty

jokes.text: Pretty much. We wrote our own vows.
jokes.setup: Asked if she promised to be miserable in marriage
jokes.punchline: Her matter-of-fact confirmation about their custom vows
jokes.explanation: Perfect character moment - casual acceptance of marital misery with darkly funny implication about their vows

jokes.text: and to think my mother had one of those.
jokes.setup: Character seeing woman's hair for first time
jokes.punchline: His disturbing comparison to his mother
jokes.explanation: Decent cringe humor but relies on uncomfortable Oedipal implications

jokes.text: And it's so suggestive. But like they say, 'snuggling sells.'
jokes.punchline: Her mangled version of 'sex sells'
jokes.explanation: Good Marge character moment - her innocent take on commercial publishing wisdom

jokes.text: Let's kiss boys. Binge and purge. Rock and roll! You're not gettin' out till we're 16!
jokes.setup: Kids getting excited about mom's book
jokes.punchline: Their escalating list of rebellious activities and Marge's reaction
jokes.explanation: Good escalation humor with appropriate parental panic response

jokes.text: Captain mordecai stared at the shop window full of powdered blowholes. 'Mmm, blowholes,' he drooled.
jokes.punchline: The obvious Homer-like behavior in the character
jokes.explanation: Good callback to Homer's food obsession through Marge's fictional character

jokes.text: here in my car  I am hosing off blood some of it's mine but most of it's not
jokes.punchline: The disturbing lyrics about blood cleanup
jokes.explanation: Good dark humor parody of Gary Numan's 'Cars' with medical incompetence theme

jokes.text: It's double-spaced. Whoo-hoo, I'm halfway through!
jokes.punchline: His excitement about double-spacing making it easier
jokes.explanation: Perfect Homer laziness - finding the easiest way to feel accomplished

jokes.text: Does anyone ever get 'tracted?' Let me call the suicide hot line and ask them.
jokes.punchline: His plan to call suicide hotline with word question
jokes.explanation: Brilliant absurdist humor - inappropriate use of crisis helpline for grammar questions

jokes.text: No! They were all, in their own way, totally awesome.
jokes.setup: Marge asking if characters bothered Homer
jokes.punchline: Homer's generic positive response reveals he didn't read it
jokes.explanation: Classic Homer fake enthusiasm to cover not doing assigned task

jokes.text: They were all, in their own way, totally awesome.
jokes.setup: Marge asking about the book characters
jokes.punchline: Homer's generic, clearly unread response
jokes.explanation: Good Homer dishonesty - his transparently fake book report language

jokes.text: As I've always said: 'Publish or perish'!
jokes.punchline: Homer using academic phrase he clearly doesn't understand
jokes.explanation: Good Homer character moment - using phrases beyond his understanding

jokes.text: Now for that happy period between the lie and the time it's found out.
jokes.setup: Homer lying about reading the book
jokes.punchline: His philosophical take on the temporary nature of deception
jokes.explanation: Excellent Homer wisdom - his surprisingly articulate and relatable take on lying

jokes.text: Thomas pynchon loved this book! Almost as much as he loves cameras.
jokes.punchline: Reference to Pynchon's famous reclusiveness
jokes.explanation: Clever literary humor about Pynchon's camera-shy reputation

jokes.text: Hell no I wouldn't ! What do you mean I just said it? That doesn't count!
jokes.setup: Clancy being tricked into giving endorsement
jokes.punchline: His protest that he didn't mean to say it
jokes.explanation: Good meta-humor about the process of creating fake endorsements

jokes.text: What if they parody it on mad tv? We're doomed.
jokes.setup: Kids worried about Dad finding out about the book
jokes.punchline: Lisa's despair at the thought of MAD TV coverage
jokes.explanation: Good meta-humor and cultural reference about media satire spreading news

jokes.text: Dr. Marvin monroe?! I haven't seen you in years. Oh, I've been very sick.
jokes.punchline: Dr. Monroe's matter-of-fact explanation for his absence
jokes.explanation: Good callback explaining character's real-world absence with in-universe reason

jokes.text: and on page 72, temperance's name changes to marge for three paragraphs.
jokes.setup: Dr. Monroe analyzing the book's parallels to real life
jokes.punchline: The smoking-gun evidence of Marge's unconscious self-insertion
jokes.explanation: Brilliant reveal showing how Marge's subconscious leaked into her writing - perfect evidence

jokes.text: And could you space out the meat so that it spells 'happy birthday, moe'?
jokes.punchline: His pathetic attempt to celebrate his own birthday
jokes.explanation: Excellent Moe characterization - his loneliness expressed through pizza customization

jokes.text: Oh, mr. Homer, anything for a cuckolded boob... I mean, uh, loyal customer.
jokes.setup: Homer asking Apu for crazy straws
jokes.punchline: Apu's slip revealing he knows about the book's implications
jokes.explanation: Good character moment showing how the book gossip has spread, with Apu's verbal slip

jokes.text: Well, then, maybe I will! If you do, you'll see she lusts for flanders.
jokes.setup: Homer threatening to read Marge's book
jokes.punchline: Apu's blunt spoiler about the Flanders parallel
jokes.explanation: Good escalation of Homer's realization with Apu's direct revelation

jokes.text: I swore never to read again after to kill a mockingbird gave me no useful advice on killing mockingbirds.
jokes.setup: Homer deciding to read Marge's book
jokes.punchline: His completely literal interpretation of the classic novel's title
jokes.explanation: Outstanding Homer moment - perfectly captures his literal mindedness and complete misunderstanding of literature. This is peak character comedy.

jokes.text: It did teach me not to judge a man based on the color of his skin, but what good does that do me?!
jokes.setup: Homer continuing about To Kill a Mockingbird
jokes.punchline: His complaint about learning tolerance as if it's useless
jokes.explanation: Excellent follow-up that shows Homer accidentally learned the book's real message but sees it as impractical

jokes.text: As read by mary-kate and ashley olsen? Take it, ashley. Bummer. I didn't knowpeople were sad in the past.
jokes.setup: The Olsen twins reading Marge's book on tape
jokes.punchline: Their Valley Girl reaction to historical sadness
jokes.explanation: Good satire of celebrity audiobook readers and their disconnect from serious content

jokes.text: You told me you liked it! You didn't rit at all! You lied to me! I didn't lie. I was writing fict with my mouth.
jokes.punchline: Homer's creative excuse about 'writing fiction with his mouth'
jokes.explanation: Brilliant Homer excuse - creative wordplay that actually makes twisted sense while being completely ridiculous

jokes.text: Well, I'll make sure you never imagine anything again!
jokes.setup: Marge explaining the romance was just imagination
jokes.punchline: Homer's threat against imagination itself
jokes.explanation: Darkly absurd threat - Homer wants to destroy Marge's imagination

jokes.text: What are you doing here? Stealing doormats.
jokes.setup: Homer confronting Flanders at his door
jokes.punchline: Homer admits to petty crime while being confrontational
jokes.explanation: Homer's casual admission of petty crime while confronting innocent Ned

jokes.text: You wouldn't kill the father of your next child, would you? Oops.
jokes.setup: Cyrus trying to talk his way out of danger
jokes.punchline: His accidental admission of the affair
jokes.explanation: Good dramatic irony - character accidentally reveals the worst possible information

jokes.text: now that your boyfriend's dead, I'm free to be selfish, drunk, emotionally distant, sexually ungenerous, pissy...
jokes.setup: Husband character after killing his rival
jokes.punchline: His comprehensive list of awful traits he can now embrace
jokes.explanation: Excellent character moment - the husband's honest self-assessment of his terrible qualities

jokes.text: flanders, pull over! I'm an ambulance! Well, he's got me there.
jokes.punchline: Flanders' logical compliance with emergency vehicle
jokes.explanation: Good character humor - Ned's law-abiding nature works against him in this situation

jokes.text: Dear lord, please make homer's blows precise and deadly with a minimum of pain. Oh, and forgive me for those impure thoughts I had about the girl on the raisin box.
jokes.punchline: His practical prayer for efficient death and confession about raisin girl
jokes.explanation: Outstanding Flanders moment - perfectly captures his character with dark humor and absurd confession. The raisin box detail is brilliant.

jokes.text: Just call me ann flanders!
jokes.setup: Homer asking Ned for relationship advice
jokes.punchline: Ned's reference to advice columnist Ann Landers
jokes.explanation: Good Ned character moment with appropriate cultural reference and wordplay on his name

jokes.text: Why would I rub her back unless I wanted to get some... oh, to make her feel good.
jokes.punchline: Homer's realization about non-sexual affection
jokes.explanation: Good Homer character development moment - his genuine realization about selfless affection

jokes.text: The end of your book was the wake-up call I needed after falling asleep at the beginning of your book.
jokes.punchline: His admission he slept through most of it
jokes.explanation: Excellent Homer line - perfectly captures his honesty within dishonesty and the ironic structure is perfect

jokes.text: Lee harvey oswald wanted to steal the jack ruby. Jack ruby was a man, not a jewel.
jokes.setup: Homer working on JFK conspiracy theory
jokes.punchline: His confusion of Jack Ruby the person with a ruby the jewel
jokes.explanation: Good Homer confusion humor showing his literal thinking applied to historical events

jokes.text: all right, we're back to square one. Put on some coffee. I was so close.
jokes.punchline: Treats major historical mystery like routine case work
jokes.explanation: Good Wiggum characterization - treating impossible cases as routine police work

tmdb_overview: Marge writes a romance novel with characters based on Homer and Ned Flanders that becomes a hit. Meanwhile, Homer gets fired from the power plant again and becomes an ambulance driver.
